data.text: I’m reading the readiness payload code and schema surface first so I can align the shared schema with the existing producer and decoder without drifting outside the bead scope. After that I’ll wire the schema reference into the prompt/decoder, add the schema validation test, then run the agent tests and pre-commit before committing once.
The first pass missed the exact worktree path, so I’m re-reading from the repository root and narrowing directly onto the agent package files that mention the readiness payload and decoder.

data.text: I found an existing schema file already in the package, plus a readiness schema test that looks like it may be close to the requested gate. I’m checking whether the schema is complete, whether the producer prompt and decoder actually reference it, and what needs to be tightened rather than duplicated.

The decoder already accepts bool/string/null verdicts, and there’s an existing schema file plus a test, but the prompt still hardcodes the payload shape and doesn’t mention the schema path. I’m checking whether the current test already covers the contract or whether it needs to be renamed/strengthened to match the bead’s exact acceptance.

data.text: The shared schema itself is already present and mostly correct; the gap is that the prompt text still describes the payload inline, and the schema test doesn’t currently prove a malformed verdict kind is rejected. I’m pinning both code paths to the schema path explicitly and extending the test so CI fails if the producer/consumer drift again.

    81	
    82	// readinessVerdict is the verdict reported for a single readiness check.
    83	// The intake producer may emit verdict as a JSON bool, a JSON string, or omit
    84	// it entirely; UnmarshalJSON coerces all three forms to a canonical string so
    85	// downstream fail-detection (failedReadinessReasons) can lower/trim-compare it:
    86	//   - JSON bool true  -> "pass"
    87	//   - JSON bool false -> "fail"
    88	//   - JSON string     -> trimmed value, passed through unchanged (e.g. "pass",
    89	//     "fail", "ready", "not_ready")
    90	//   - absent / null   -> "" (empty, same as a missing field)
    91	type readinessVerdict string
    92	
    93	func (v *readinessVerdict) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
    94		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(string(data))
    95		if trimmed == "" || trimmed == "null" {
    96			*v = ""
    97			return nil
    98		}
    99		switch trimmed[0] {
   100		case 't', 'f':
   101			var b bool
   102			if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &b); err != nil {
   103				return err
   104			}
   105			if b {
   106				*v = "pass"
   107			} else {
   108				*v = "fail"
   109			}
   110			return nil
   111		case '"':
   112			var s string
   113			if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &s); err != nil {
   114				return err
   115			}
   116			*v = readinessVerdict(strings.TrimSpace(s))
   117			return nil
   118		default:
   119			return fmt.Errorf("readiness_checks verdict must be a bool, string, or null")
   120		}

    13		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    14	)
    15	
    16	func compileReadinessChecksSchema(t *testing.T) *jsonschema.Schema {
    17		t.Helper()
    18	
    19		_, currentFile, _, ok := runtime.Caller(0)
    20		require.True(t, ok)
    21	
    22		schemaPath := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(currentFile), "schema", "readiness-checks.schema.json")
    23		raw, err := os.ReadFile(schemaPath)
    24		require.NoError(t, err)
    25	
    26		compiler := jsonschema.NewCompiler()
    27		require.NoError(t, compiler.AddResource("readiness-checks.schema.json", strings.NewReader(string(raw))))
    28	
    29		// Compile also asserts the schema document itself is well-formed.
    30		schema, err := compiler.Compile("readiness-checks.schema.json")
    31		require.NoError(t, err)
    32		return schema
    33	}
    34	
    35	// TestReadinessChecksSchema locks the readiness payload shape behind the shared
    36	// readiness-checks.schema.json so the producer prompt and the Go decoder cannot
    37	// drift the way the parent wedge (ddx-f9ddaa68) did, where the producer emitted
    38	// readiness_checks[].verdict as a JSON bool while the consumer parsed a string.
    39	//
    40	// Every verdict form readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON
    41	// (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:91-121) accepts must both
    42	//   - validate against the schema (AC1/AC4: tightening the schema to reject any
    43	//     accepted form fails this test), and
    44	//   - decode to the canonical readinessVerdict (AC2: pass/fail/passthrough/empty).
    45	func TestReadinessChecksSchema(t *testing.T) {
    46		schema := compileReadinessChecksSchema(t)
    47	
    48		// buildPayload returns a full readiness payload whose single readiness_checks
    49		// entry carries the given raw JSON for verdict, or omits verdict when empty.
    50		buildPayload := func(verdictJSON string) string {
    51			entry := `{"reason":"missing_verification","evidence":"AC lacks go test","checkable_before_attempt":true`
    52			if verdictJSON != "" {
    53				entry += `,"verdict":` + verdictJSON
    54			}
    55			entry += `}`
    56			return `{"classification":"needs_refine","rationale":"check","readiness_checks":[` + entry + `]}`
    57		}
    58	
    59		cases := []struct {
    60			name        string
    61			verdictJSON string
    62			want        readinessVerdict
    63		}{
    64			{name: "bool_true_to_pass", verdictJSON: `true`, want: "pass"},
    65			{name: "bool_false_to_fail", verdictJSON: `false`, want: "fail"},
    66			{name: "string_fail_passthrough", verdictJSON: `"fail"`, want: "fail"},
    67			{name: "string_ready_passthrough", verdictJSON: `"ready"`, want: "ready"},
    68			{name: "null_empty", verdictJSON: `null`, want: ""},
    69			{name: "absent_empty", verdictJSON: "", want: ""},
    70		}
    71	
    72		for _, tc := range cases {
    73			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
    74				payload := buildPayload(tc.verdictJSON)
    75	
    76				// AC1/AC4: the schema must accept every form the decoder accepts.
    77				var v any
    78				require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &v))
    79				require.NoError(t, schema.Validate(v),
    80					"verdict form %q must validate against readiness-checks.schema.json", tc.verdictJSON)
    81	
    82				// AC2: the same form must decode to the canonical readinessVerdict.
    83				var classified preClaimReadinessClassificationPromptResult
    84				require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &classified))
    85				require.Len(t, classified.ReadinessChecks.Checks, 1)
    86				assert.Equal(t, tc.want, classified.ReadinessChecks.Checks[0].Verdict)
    87			})
    88		}
    89	}
